description_long: This object consists of a group of four angular, faceted stones of varying sizes, arranged closely together. The stones have a geometric, low-poly appearance with sharp edges and flat surfaces, giving them a stylized, modern look. The largest stone is upright and blocky, while the others are smaller and more irregular, with one stone partially leaning against the largest. The surfaces are uniformly colored in shades of light to medium gray, with no visible veining, carvings, or natural weathering. The stones appear solid and heavy, with matte, non-reflective finishes that suggest a hard, durable material. This arrangement would be typical as a decorative or functional barrier in an urban park, plaza, or landscaped sidewalk.
description: A cluster of four angular, faceted gray stones with a modern, geometric appearance and matte finish. The stones vary in size and are closely grouped, forming a solid, stylized barrier.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cluster, with the largest upright stone on the right and three smaller stones grouped to the left, all with sharp, flat surfaces.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlights the angular geometry and the way the stones nest together, with the largest stone dominating the composition.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the blocky backs of the two largest stones and the relative size difference between them, with the smallest stone partially obscured.
description_view_3: Oblique view emphasizes the overlapping arrangement and the faceted surfaces, showing the stones' depth and how they lean against each other.
attribute_location: urban park, landscaped plaza, or sidewalk edge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped as a decorative barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: No visible pattern; surfaces are uniformly colored and faceted with no veining or carvings
attribute_weathering: Surfaces appear freshly cut and unweathered, with sharp edges and no signs of erosion or lichen
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with man-made structures but can blend with other decorative stones or landscaping elements; typically placed on open ground or among plants
